description: Live ledger for recording autobuild tranche executions, evidence, closure state, and the next recommended patch.
instructions: Append one entry per closed or actively-tracked autobuild tranche.
Prefer source-backed evidence such as commits, CI runs, verification outputs, or durable docs artifacts.
Do not record workstation-only or user-home-only cleanup here unless the tranche was explicitly an environment-maintenance pass.

trigger_signal.source: <resource, report, CI run, or human request>

changes: <what changed>
stop_condition: <when to stop instead of expanding scope>

evidence.summary: <what this evidence proves>

trigger_signal.summary: Create durable human-readable and machine-readable planning surfaces for ralphglasses autobuild patch selection and future-session recovery.

changes: added the human-readable autobuild patch queue
added the machine-readable autobuild patch queue
added a schema for the autobuild patch queue
added autobuild cycle improvement notes
added a machine-readable autobuild execution ledger template
added a schema for the autobuild execution ledger
seeded the first live autobuild execution ledger
acceptance_condition: future sessions have a stable markdown queue, JSON queue, and schema
future sessions have a repo-owned note lane for patch selection lessons
future sessions have a machine-readable ledger format for tranche execution evidence
stop_condition: stop after the queue, notes, template, schema, and first live ledger all exist on main
do not expand into broader roadmap linking or code changes in the same tranche

evidence.summary: Added the autobuild patch queue markdown checkpoint.
Added the machine-readable autobuild patch queue.
Added the autobuild patch queue schema.
Added autobuild cycle improvement notes.
Added the autobuild execution ledger template.
Added the autobuild execution ledger schema.

result.summary: Ralphglasses now has a repo-owned planning bundle for autobuild tranche selection, operational notes, and machine-readable tranche recording.

notes: The next highest-leverage follow-up is to link these artifacts from README.md and ROADMAP.md so they become canonical entrypoints instead of discoverable-only docs.
Further roadmap work should remain narrow and integrity-led rather than reopening broad filesystem-hardening sweeps.

trigger_signal.summary: The ranked autobuild queue selected tracked temp artifacts and placeholder outputs as the next small repo-owned gate after the short-suite repair.

changes: added a tracked artifact gate that scans tracked paths via git ls-files
added an explicit repo-owned allowlist for justified artifact exceptions
added shell fixture tests for temp artifacts and placeholder filenames
wired the artifact gate into scripts/dev/ci.sh and scripts/dev/pre-commit
acceptance_condition: repo-owned verification fails when tracked temp artifacts or placeholder outputs are present
the failure names the offending path without requiring manual review
stop_condition: stop after deterministic path-based checks, allowlist support, and fixture coverage exist
do not expand this tranche into broader content linting or unrelated cleanup

evidence.summary: Introduces the tracked-path artifact gate.
Covers .DS_Store, temp directory, placeholder filename, and allowlist behavior.
Runs the artifact gate before the longer CI phases.

result.summary: Ralphglasses now fails fast on tracked temp artifacts and placeholder outputs before deeper verification runs.
prevented_failure_class: late detection of source-tree hygiene failures

notes: The gate intentionally uses path and filename contracts instead of content greps to avoid false positives in docs, fixtures, and examples.

trigger_signal.summary: Full CI on current main reproduced provider-role projection drift via scripts/sync-provider-roles.py --check after the artifact-gate tranche landed.

changes: regenerated the stale .claude and .gemini provider role projections
revalidated scripts/sync-provider-roles.py --check on current main
reran the full scripts/dev/ci.sh commit gate after regeneration
acceptance_condition: python3 scripts/sync-provider-roles.py --check passes on current main
scripts/dev/ci.sh returns green after the provider projection resync
stop_condition: stop after the generated projections are resynced and the publish lane is green again
do not expand the tranche into unrelated role-manifest editing or broader queue work

evidence.summary: Representative regenerated Claude provider projection.
Representative regenerated Gemini provider projection.
Full commit gate passed after the projection sync.

result.summary: Ralphglasses returned to a green commit gate by resyncing provider role projections that had drifted on current main.
prevented_failure_class: generated provider surface drift on main
red publish lane inherited by unrelated later tranches

notes: The fix deliberately stayed generated-only; no role manifest source changed in this tranche.

trigger_signal.summary: The ranked autobuild queue selected the top-level TUI layout harness because nested viewport-backed views only had broad snapshots and could drift on width handling without a focused gate.

changes: added top-level teatest golden snapshots for nested Repo Detail and Fleet views
added explicit visible-width budget assertions for those nested views
added a resize-path width-budget regression test for nested views
sorted repo detail config keys before rendering so golden snapshots are deterministic
acceptance_condition: nested layout width regressions become deterministic test failures
the known top-level TUI harness exercises nested Repo Detail and Fleet views instead of only overview and help surfaces
stop_condition: stop after the nested TUI harness has deterministic snapshots and width-budget assertions
do not expand this tranche into broader visual redesign or unrelated TUI cleanup

evidence.summary: Adds nested-view teatest fixtures, width-budget assertions, and resize coverage.
Sorts repo detail config keys to remove snapshot nondeterminism.
Captures the nested Repo Detail view through the top-level TUI harness.
Captures the nested Fleet view through the top-level TUI harness.
Verifies the new layout harness gate alongside the broader TUI packages.

result.summary: Ralphglasses now fails deterministically when nested Repo Detail or Fleet layouts drift past their width budget, and the repo detail snapshot no longer depends on map iteration order.
prevented_failure_class: nested TUI width drift landing unnoticed
late discovery of viewport-backed layout regressions

notes: The gate intentionally covers one detail view and one dashboard view through the real top-level layout path rather than adding another broad UI snapshot sweep.

trigger_signal.summary: The ranked autobuild queue selected the remaining repo-owned path bypass set after the broader filesystem-hardening work had already landed, with the remaining risk concentrated in active callers still using inline path joins.

repo_owned_scope: active command and runtime path callers
changes: added shared helpers for cost events, command history, and external session search paths
moved session, budget, and tenant command discovery onto the shared search helper with legacy scan-root fallback
removed the marathon command's direct scan-root session-state override so new runtime state stays on the canonical shared path
added regression coverage for helper resolution and shared-plus-legacy external session discovery
acceptance_condition: active config, state, and session-adjacent callers resolve through shared helpers or an explicit compatibility helper
legacy scan-root external sessions remain discoverable while the shared session state dir stays canonical
stop_condition: stop after the remaining active bypasses are centralized and covered by targeted regression tests
do not reopen a broader filesystem sweep unless new repo-owned bypasses are discovered

evidence.summary: Centralizes the remaining cost-event, command-history, and external-session path contracts.
Routes CLI external-session discovery through the shared search helper.
Verifies shared and legacy scan-root external sessions are both discoverable.
Verifies the path-helper tranche across the affected packages.

result.summary: Ralphglasses now resolves the remaining active path bypasses through shared helpers, keeps legacy scan-root external session discovery explicit, and stops new runtime state from landing in ad hoc session-state locations.
prevented_failure_class: active path policy drift hidden in inline string joins
split command discovery between shared and legacy session locations
repeat path-hardening sweeps to chase a small residual bypass set

notes: The tranche intentionally stayed within active repo-owned callers and did not treat workspace docs-root discovery as shared-path debt.

trigger_signal.summary: The ranked autobuild queue selected the missing selector layer because the repo already exposed discovery-adoption and adoption-priority fronts but still lacked one machine-readable summary that translated those signals into concrete patch ids.

changes: added a machine-readable autobuild tranche selector that ranks concrete patch ids from the live adoption fronts
exposed the selector through a new catalog resource
wired the selector into catalog-server and runtime-health summaries for read-first discovery
added regression coverage for the selector summary, new resource registration, and updated resource counts
acceptance_condition: one machine-readable summary ranks the next repo-owned patch candidates
the summary includes source signal, recommended entry surface, and confidence
stop_condition: stop after the selector exists as a read-first resource and the affected packages pass verification
do not expand this tranche into repairing the next selected patch in the same scope wave

evidence.summary: Introduces the autobuild tranche selector and its patch-ranking logic.
Registers the new autobuild tranche resource in the discovery contract.
Verifies the new resource and updated static resource registration.
Verifies the combined selector, path-helper, and TUI-harness tranches across the affected packages.

result.summary: Ralphglasses now has a machine-readable adoption-led selector that ranks concrete autobuild patch ids with source signal, confidence, and entry surface; the current top handoff is remote_main_red_signal_filter.
prevented_failure_class: adoption telemetry without a tranche-selection consumer
manual translation from workflow gaps to patch ids
static queue handoffs ignoring live repo signals

notes: The selector deliberately ranks repo-owned patch ids instead of raw workflows so future autobuild loops can hand off directly into implementation.
